features.name: Rich Function View
features.description: Interactive UI for executing any registered function or script as a model, with auto-generated inputs, run controls, and structured output panels. Central model-execution surface of the legacy Compute framework.
features.evidence_summary: openModelFromFuncall and RichFunctionViewEditor share the same funccall→view pipeline and are directly explained by the dedicated Rich Function View guide and the top-level Compute/Models docs.

features.name: Scripting Platform
features.description: Author and run multi-language scripts (R, Python, JS) inside Datagrok, with a rich script editor, custom input widgets, REST invocation, and visualisation of outputs.
features.evidence_summary: All docs under /help/compute/scripting/ form a coherent scripting guide hierarchy; CustomStringInput is a custom widget used within scripting UIs; the two sample scripts demonstrate the platform's default view and stats capabilities.

features.name: Diff Studio ODE Models
features.description: Solve and visualise systems of ordinary differential equations interactively using Diff Studio, with bundled canonical examples covering chaotic systems and ecological models.
features.evidence_summary: Logistic Map, Lorenz Attractor, and Lotka-Volterra are all classic ODE/dynamical-system examples that directly illustrate Diff Studio's ODE simulation capability, co-documented in the Diff Studio guide.

features.name: Object Cooling Simulation Demo
features.description: Interactive Newton's law of cooling simulation with per-parameter validators and a selector for cooling mode, serving as a self-contained demo of the Compute model framework.
features.evidence_summary: Five Validators (SimTime, DesiredTemp, InitialTemp, AmbTemp, HeatCap), ObjectCoolingSelector, and the Object cooling script share a common 'cooling simulation' naming root and domain; Heat Exchange is a related thermal simulation.

features.name: Model Optimization Tools
features.description: Parameter fitting, sensitivity analysis, and outlier detection tooling for Compute models; core fitting and SA capabilities have migrated to Compute2 but documentation remains in this package.
features.evidence_summary: The function-analysis guide covers model optimization (sensitivity analysis, fitting, outlier detection), and OutliersSelectionViewer is described as an 'outliers selection viewer' — closely related tooling confirmed by changelog entries for SA and Fitting.

features.name: Custom Data Upload
features.description: Extensible widget and handler for uploading custom input data within a model or pipeline run, allowing users to provide non-standard data types via a configurable uploader UI.
features.evidence_summary: CustomDataUploader, CustomUploader (widget returning funccall), and CustomCustomizer share the 'Custom*' name prefix and the same data-upload / customisation domain.

features.name: Pipeline Step Editor
features.description: Editor component that wraps a single function call as a step inside a multi-step model pipeline, enabling chaining of compute operations.
features.evidence_summary: PipelineStepEditor takes a funccall and produces an object, matching the pattern of a pipeline composition primitive; the changelog references pipeline and browse-view rewrites.

features.name: Jupyter Notebook Integration
features.description: Run and display Jupyter notebooks as compute artefacts within the Datagrok platform, bridging notebook-based workflows with the model execution UI.
features.evidence_summary: A dedicated user-facing guide exists for Jupyter Notebook integration under the /help/compute/ hierarchy, indicating it is a standalone user-facing capability.

features.name: Biomedical Example Scripts
features.description: Domain-specific demonstration scripts covering ECG interval analysis, antibody Fab arm exchange, and bioreactor filter sizing (Vmax), showcasing Compute's multi-language scripting for life-sciences applications.
features.evidence_summary: IntervalsFromECG (cardiology), Fab Arm Exchange (antibody biology), and Vmax (bioprocess engineering) are all life-sciences domain scripts that don't fit other feature clusters but form a coherent 'biomedical demos' group.
